title: Question: Why isn't the subuid in the Buildah containerfile set to 1001:65535?
body:
Subuid 1001:64535 is configured in the reference to buildah:

https://github.com/containers/image_build/blob/main/buildah/Containerfile#L95

but the maximum user id should be 65535. Why is there no set 1000 uid after leaving?

Looking forward to a reply.

comments.body:
I'm not sure I understand your question, but:
* The defaults for `useradd` would assign UID 1000 as the primary ID for the `build` user being created on the previous line, so it shouldn't be assigned as subuid.
* The third field for a line in `/etc/subuid` is the size of the range, not the value at the end of the range, so the "build" user is being assigned the range starting at and including 1001 up to and including 65535, which tracks since we're guessing that if the image is run in a user namespace, it'll be given 65536 IDs (in a range from 0 to 65535) to work with.
* I can't say with authority why there's no `USER 1000` in the Containerfile (that's more of a question to be posed in that repository), but I can guess that doing so would have made it harder for unprivileged users to use the image.

> I'm not sure I understand your question, but:
> 
> * The defaults for `useradd` would assign UID 1000 as the primary ID for the `build` user being created on the previous line, so it shouldn't be assigned as subuid.
> * The third field for a line in `/etc/subuid` is the size of the range, not the value at the end of the range, so the "build" user is being assigned the range starting at and including 1001 up to and including 65535, which tracks since we're guessing that if the image is run in a user namespace, it'll be given 65536 IDs (in a range from 0 to 65535) to work with.
> * I can't say with authority why there's no `USER 1000` in the Containerfile (that's more of a question to be posed in that repository), but I can guess that doing so would have made it harder for unprivileged users to use the image.

thinks for reply. I understand the question
